methods.description: Procedure for collecting microalgae that migrate to the sediment surface during simulated low tide using lens tissue.

steps.description: Water was drained from the trays on the day after the sampling.
steps.searchable_summary: Drain water from experimental trays containing sediment.

steps.description: 3 h prior to the expected low tide we added two layers of lens tissue on top of the sediment.
steps.searchable_summary: Add lens tissue layers to the surface of drained sediment.
outputs: trays with sediment and lens tissue

steps.description: The sediment was then exposed to natural light from a window to facilitate biofilm vertical migration.
steps.searchable_summary: Expose sediment with lens tissue to natural light.
inputs: trays with sediment and lens tissue

steps.description: Lens tissues were recovered at the middle of the virtual emersion time (low-tide peak), thereby harvesting the microalgae that had migrated to the sediment surface during the first 3 h (Eaton and Moss, 1966).
steps.searchable_summary: Recover lens tissues containing migrated microalgae.
inputs: trays exposed to light with sediment and lens tissue

steps.description: Cells were extracted from the lens tissues by gently scrapping them into site-filtered sea water.
steps.searchable_summary: Extract cells from lens tissues by scraping into filtered seawater.

steps.description: and kept in darkness before further use.
steps.searchable_summary: Store the extracted algal suspension in darkness.

methods.description: Procedure for preparing experimental microplates with either mud or lens tissue, inoculated with harvested algal suspension, for subsequent experiments.

steps.description: On the second day, 5 h before expected low tide and 2 h before the beginning of the experiment 48 black microplates wells (22 mm diameter and 18 mm depth) were filled with either the same mud used for extraction or clean lens tissue inoculated with algal suspension.
steps.searchable_summary: Fill 48 microplate wells with either mud or clean lens tissue.
outputs: microplates filled with mud or lens tissue

steps.description: Half were filled with one lens tissue layer and inoculated with 2 mL of algal suspension; (Mud samples contained both migratory and non-migratory diatoms from the field. Half were filled with one lens tissue layer and inoculated with 2 mL of algal suspension; the other half was filled with mud (15 mm) and 0.5 mL of algal suspension. To facilitate handling the 12 wells microplates were cut in rows of 4 wells containing each 3 replicates and a dark control (Figure 1). Mud and lens tissue samples will henceforth respectively be referred as migrational and non-migrational biofilms.)
steps.searchable_summary: Inoculate half of the lens tissue filled wells with 2 mL of algal suspension (non-migrational biofilms).

steps.description: the other half was filled with mud (15 mm) and 0.5 mL of algal suspension. (Mud samples contained both migratory and non-migratory diatoms from the field. Half were filled with one lens tissue layer and inoculated with 2 mL of algal suspension; the other half was filled with mud (15 mm) and 0.5 mL of algal suspension. To facilitate handling the 12 wells microplates were cut in rows of 4 wells containing each 3 replicates and a dark control (Figure 1). Mud and lens tissue samples will henceforth respectively be referred as migrational and non-migrational biofilms.)
steps.searchable_summary: Inoculate the other half of the mud filled wells (15 mm depth) with 0.5 mL of algal suspension (migrational biofilms).

steps.description: To facilitate handling the 12 wells microplates were cut in rows of 4 wells containing each 3 replicates and a dark control (Figure 1).
steps.searchable_summary: Cut 12-well microplates into rows of 4 wells (3 replicates + 1 dark control).
